codeReview.summary: The commit correctly moves issue-list and issue-detail company-scoped queries onto the route/deep-linked company, and the new tests cover the stale-selected-company read/upload path. However, both pages now perform their own unconditional route-sync writes back into `selectedCompanyId`, which bypasses the existing manual-switch guard and can snap the operator back to the stale route company before remembered-path navigation completes.

issues.description: This new effect unconditionally calls `setSelectedCompanyId(routeCompanyId, { source: "route_sync" })` whenever the prefixed route and current selection differ. That bypasses the shared guard in `ui/src/lib/company-selection.ts:3-16`, whose explicit purpose is to defer route sync while a manual company switch is in flight, and it can race `useCompanyPageMemory()` (`ui/src/hooks/useCompanyPageMemory.ts:68-74`) when the operator switches companies from `/ALP/issues`. In that flow the switcher sets `selectionSource` to `manual`, but this page can immediately overwrite the new selection back to Alpha before the remembered-path redirect to Beta finishes, breaking manual company switching from the issue list.

The detail page repeats the same direct route-sync write with `setSelectedCompanyId(nextCompanyId, { source: "route_sync" })` instead of going through the existing `shouldSyncCompanySelectionFromRoute()` logic used by `ui/src/components/Layout.tsx:138-144`. As a result, a manual company switch started from a deep-linked issue page like `/ALP/issues/ALP-1` can be reverted back to the route company before `useCompanyPageMemory()` navigates to the newly selected company's remembered page, so this fix trades the stale-query bug for a new company-switch regression on issue detail surfaces.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Route-driven company selection already has a shared guard (`shouldSyncCompanySelectionFromRoute`) to protect manual company switches, but the new issue pages duplicated route-sync behavior instead of reusing that helper.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: `ui/src/lib/company-selection.ts:3-16` and `ui/src/components/Layout.tsx:138-144` define the intended route-sync rule; the reviewed commit adds direct `setSelectedCompanyId(..., { source: "route_sync" })` calls in `ui/src/pages/Issues.tsx:91` and `ui/src/pages/IssueDetail.tsx:802`.

summary: Reviewed the mission feature metadata, worker handoff, transcript skeleton, `web-runtime-worker` skill, architecture guidance, and commit `92e05d294e3292ff4def82568bcb24f8ec3212a2`. The feature should not pass as-is: while it fixes stale company-scoped issue queries, it also bypasses the existing manual-switch route-sync guard on both issue list and issue detail pages, introducing a blocking regression for switching companies from those surfaces.
